name: task-log
title: task-log
desc: Claude logs every task it completes to .claude/tasks.json — so you see at a glance what was done in a project, without asking.

name: gitmir-model
title: gitmir-model
desc: Builds a living, id-linked model of your product in .gitmir/model/ (data, server logic, API, frontend, events, processes, lifecycles). Claude — and you — grasp the whole product without re-reading the code.
prepend: Build (or, if a model already exists, idempotently update it, keeping stable ids) the multidimensional object model of THIS project by following the specification below in full — including the mandatory standing rule it defines (install/refresh the 'GitMir model — source of truth' section in the project's CLAUDE.md). Write only inside .gitmir/ and that one CLAUDE.md section; do not modify application source.

name: context-distillation
title: context-distillation
desc: Distils a pile of docs, tickets or chat threads into a small, checkable brief in .gitmir/brief.json — the minimal context Claude needs, and the artifact people can confirm before anything is built.

name: task-planner
title: task-planner
desc: Turns a goal into small, self-contained task files in tasks/todo/, each carrying the right model context — ready for the runner to execute one by one.

name: task-runner
title: task-runner
desc: Works the task queue autonomously — takes each task, does it, and moves the file todo → in progress → done. Batch work without babysitting.

name: legacy-maintenance
title: legacy-maintenance
desc: Change or maintain an existing legacy codebase without breaking the behaviour next to it — maps the blast radius from the model, then ships small reversible steps you can verify. For keeping old projects alive, not rewriting them.

name: stack-port
title: stack-port
desc: Port an old, hand-written project onto a new stack with full feature parity — the old app is the spec, a parity ledger keeps anything from being silently dropped, and every slice is verified against the old behaviour, not just a green build.
